Risk Title: High Defect Reopen Rate and QA Budget Overrun
Risk Desc: QA testing across all sprints shows medium defect leakage with multiple defects requiring 2-3 retesting cycles. Budget exceeded by ₹25,000 (8.3% overrun) due to increased retesting efforts. Defects reopened due to incomplete fixes, causing delivery delays and resource strain.
Potential Impact: Reduced software reliability, increased cost, delayed delivery, customer dissatisfaction

Risk Title: QA Automation Gap and Manual Testing Dependency
Risk Desc: Divya joined mid-sprint (Sprint 4) to improve QA automation coverage and defect revalidation. Despite addition, QA budget still exceeded due to manual testing dependency and regression turnaround time. Automation gap indicates technical debt and process inefficiency.
Potential Impact: Extended testing cycles, manual effort dependency, scalability issues, quality risk

Risk Desc: JIRA data shows medium defect leakage across sprints with reopen counts ranging from 0-2 per issue. Multiple defects required 2-3 retesting cycles indicating incomplete fixes and quality process gaps. Defect leakage exceeding 3% threshold poses reliability and customer satisfaction risks.
Potential Impact: Customer dissatisfaction, production defects, reliability issues, warranty costs, reputation damage

Risk Title: Extended Regression Testing Turnaround Impacting Sprint Velocity
Risk Desc: Divya's addition in Sprint 4 aimed to improve regression turnaround time. However, QA budget overrun and continued In Testing status across sprints indicate extended regression cycles. Regression bottleneck impacts sprint velocity and go-live readiness.
Potential Impact: Sprint velocity reduction, go-live delays, quality risk, team frustration, cascading impacts
